rationale.hitl-workflow: Developers retain approval, steer incrementally, and verify agent outputs.
rationale.risk-routing: Task complexity and stakes determine delegation and required human attention.
rationale.agent-scope-drift: Agents exceed requests; developers constrain tasks, context, dependencies, and changes.
rationale.rules-based-checks: Developers validate changes using tests, linters, execution, and version control.
rationale.quality-debt: Participants report messy code, overengineering, integration failures, and maintainability concerns.
rationale.ai-review: Developers use agents for code review, while reporting mixed reliability.
rationale.survey-input: Survey quantifies professional practices, preferences, task suitability, and oversight attitudes.
rationale.problem-statement-anchor: No respondent considered agents suitable for replacing human decision-making.
